desc:
The Greenway stretches onward into the distance, both north and south, as it
makes its way through an area of open plains. On either side of the road, a flat
expanse of grassy, and otherwise quite featureless land is all that can be seen.
name: Greenway

desc:
The road leads ever on and on. As far as you see, the road runs straight north
and south. The soil is hard, beaten down over the centuries by the passage of
multitudes of wagons and horses. To the west, beyond a now weed-filled field, a
broken down farm house can be seen.
name: Greenway

desc:
The large, wide road runs from north to south, like a deep scar through the
countryside. To the west, a small, stony trail tracks off through a weed- filled
field, toward an obviously ruined farm, which stands amid the endless plains.
name: Greenway

desc:
The Greenway bends east and north here as it makes its way through the
countryside. The hard-packed earth of the roadway bears a few ruts and holes,
but is quite easy to travel. To either side of the road, beyond a steep
embankment, a vast expanse of plains can be seen, and to the south, a small
building can be seen in the distance.
name: Greenway

desc:
The road bends west and south here, cutting its way across the seemingly endless
plains, which lie beyond a steep embankment, to either side. Far to the
southwest, a small building stands near to the road, and in the northwest, a
small farm can be seen.
name: Greenway

desc:
The road stretches more or less straight north and south here. Hard-packed soil
has been marked by merchant carriages and horses travelling from Bree to Tharbad
trough the ages. Just west you see a ruined building. A cold gust of wind whips
at you.
name: Greenway

desc:
The mighty Greenway bends north and east here, cleaving through the dry fields
bordering the road. The soil has been trampled hard by wheels, boots and
horseshoes, providing an easy way from Bree to Tharbad.
name: Greenway

desc:
This much used road bends west and south here, in its journey between Bree and
Tharbad. The ground is made up of nothing more than dirt and stones, packed hard
by the passage of innumerable wagons and horses through the ages. To either side
of the roadway, stretching out into the distance, lie desolate fields and
plains. A little further to the south, the road bends around a small hill.
name: Greenway

desc:
You wander on the Greenway, a large road, and judging from the soil, much used
by travellers on horse and wagons. The road bends north and east here. Westward,
a flat field is unfolding, while to the south you see what appears to be the
highest point around, yet little more than a small hill at best.
name: Greenway

desc:
The road bends west and south here, while to the north and northeast a rather
dry and dismal field is unfolding. The road is clearly scarred by multitudes of
merchant caravans travelling through these areas. A small hill rises up to the
southwest.
name: Greenway

desc:
This large road leads almost straight north and south. The soil is hard and dry,
clearly marked by the passage of thousands of wagons and horses. The road leads
north as far as can be seen, while just south, the road joins with two other
roads, making a junction. A small hill can be seen to the west, providing a
small break in this rather flat area.
name: Greenway

desc:
You find yourself at a junction of three roads. A much travelled road, trodden
down by the passages of merchant caravans from Bree to Tharbad, bends north and
east here. A smaller road stretches westwards. A small river runs along the road
west and east. A small hill can be seen to the northwest. A rotten signpost can
be seen at the crossing, barely standing.
name: The Crossroad

desc:
You walk along a desolate road. A bit small for a road, yet too wide to be a
path, it seems to stretch on to the horizon westwards. To the east you see it
joining with another road, forming a T-junction. This road seems seldom
travelled, though the road bears old marks of horses and wagons. To the south a
small river flows eastward. A small hill can be seen to the northeast.
name: Old Road

desc:
You wander along a dry and desolate road. Although calling it a road may be an
overstatement, it is far too wide to be called a path. It appears seldom used,
seldom travelled, yet judging from the hard trodden soil, it must once have been
much used. To the west you see the road bending north, while to the east you see
the road continues more or less straight on. You glimpse what might be a
crossroad far to the east, with a small hill north of it.
name: Old Road

desc:
The road bends north and east, following the curves of the river to the south.
The river seems to have dug into the earth, leaving the edges of the river
clearcut. At the bend of the river, you see a rather large black stone.
name: Old Road

desc:
The road turns south and west here. Although apparently seldom travelled
nowadays, the surface of the road is hard-packed, and marked by the passage of
many wagons and horse across the years. But parched weeds have now begun to
force their way through the soil, slowly destroying the road. Plains can be seen
to the northeast, a river follows the road to the south- west, and a small
forest can be seen far southwest.
name: Old Road

desc:
This road must have been much used in ages gone, the soil beaten down by the
wheels of wagons and the hooves of horses. Weeds now grows where man used to
walk, growing, yet choked by the hard-packed dirt. The river to the south still
follows the road. Plains unfold to the northeast, while a small forest can be
seen to the southwest. Some of the trees seems to grow higher than the others.
name: Old Road

desc:
This narrow roadway bends to the north and east here, faithfully following the
course of the small river, to the south. Although old, and now obviously falling
into disuse and disrepair, the surface of the road is still quite flat and even,
the dirt and stones packed down hard by the passage of wagon, hoof and foot,
over many long years of use. Plains stretch out to the north- east, while a
small forest can be seen to the southwest. Some of the trees there seems higher
than the others, providing a good place to check out the area.
name: Old Road

desc:
The road runs north and south. It appears seldom travelled, yet the road has
been trampled hard, clearly marked with the passage of some wagons and horses. A
forest can be seen to the southwest, with some rather large trees sticking up,
providing a good view of this area.
name: Old Road

desc:
A small dirt road cuts a path through the countryside, turning here from the
south to the west. Eastward, beyond a tall and sickly hedgerow, a vast, barren
plain opens out before you, whilst to the south, in the far distance, the road
meets a river.
name: Old Road

desc:
This road must once have been well travelled, the ground is packed hard. Yet the
weeds pushing their way through the soil signals the end of this road, dooming
it to vanish in history. The road follows the contours of the land, running more
or less straight east and west.
name: Old Road

desc:
The surface of the road is rather muddy here, although the reason for this is
not readily apparent. However, the hedgerow to the south has taken on a
different character, being comprised here mainly of tall, bright green ferns.
name: Old Road

desc:
You follow a small road through these dismal plains. The road bends west and
south here, and you can barely discern a faint path diverging from the road,
leading north to a small hill in the middle of a large plain.
name: Old Road

desc:
This is the beginning of the Road to Tharbad, a town known in rumours as the
city of thieves and murderers. Even with its dark reputation, the road shows
signs of regular use, with the indentions from merchant caravans and the tracks
of shod horses. Just to the south you see a river, which travels beside the road
for some distance, and to the northwest, stands a small hill, which is the
highest point in this area.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road travels on straight to the east and the west. To the north, a great
expanse of barren plains seems to stretch endlessly before you, and the road
upon which you stand is scarred by many tracks, which speak of frequent, and
recent, heavy use, by both vehicles and mounted travellers.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
You are travelling along a well-tended road, bordered to the south by some light
woods, and from somewhere nearby, the sound of birdsong can be heard. The road
stretches, seemingly without end, to both the east and west.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road turns sharply to the south here, before continuing its journey to the
east, toward the city of Tharbad. A small dirt trail can be seen to the north,
parting ways with the road, to descend a steep and stony embankment.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
Rocks and boulders are scattered all around, amongst the thin tufts of grass,
and clumps of weeds. Little other vegetation seems able to grow here, though a
few stunted bushes seem to cling stubbornly to life. To the north and south,
these desolate plains stretch endlessly onward.
name: Dry Plain

desc:
A large boulder stands here, amid these desolate plains. Whichever way you turn,
the plains stretch endlessly onward, dominating the landscape for many miles.
The grass grows in thin, sickly tufts here, and only a few stunted bushes manage
to survive in this barren region.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
These plains are the essence of desolation. The only vegetation surviving here
is the occasional weed, except for a large group of bushes, which seem quite out
of place here. Indeed, alone in all this are, this clump of bushes seems to be
fairly healthy, even thriving.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
These desolate plains are almost featureless, save for an occasional rock, or a
small stand of weeds. Whichever way you turn your gaze, this picture of
devastation is mirrored, and nothing stands in the way of the cold, harsh winds,
which whip across the landscape.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
Desolation is the only word for these plains. Even the weeds seems to have
problems with surviving, but here and there you see an occasional flower or a
bush, straining to make these plains fruitful, as they must once have been. Yet
that seems to be a futile battle. The desolation can be seen in all directions.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
Stiff blades of dead grass still stand here. Perhaps this ground was once rich
and fertile, but it is now unable to sustain plant life. The earth is parched
and cracked, hard underfoot, offering little bounce to your step.
name: Dead Grass Plains

desc:
Standing in the middle of this dreary plain, the rare large boulder and the odd
bush is all that can be seen in any direction. Gusts of wind whips at
everything, wearing down what little that refuses to conform to flatness.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
Gazing eastward reveals a scene of utter desolation. In that direction, a vast
expanse of barren and seemingly lifeless plains opens out before you. Westward,
the Greenway cleaves a north to south path through the plain, as it journeys
between Bree and Tharbad.
name: Barren Plain

desc:
These dreary plains would bring tears to the eyes of any farmer. Little but
weeds could thrive here, yet you see the occasional dry bush striving to
survive. All that can be seen, is nothing but these same plains unfolding.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
These flatlands are totally devoid of any plant life. The area appears never to
have been cultivated, and the dirt is hard-packed and cracked, as if it had not
seen water in years. Each footfall makes a peculiar crunching noise on the hard
ground.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
The plain rises steeply to the east, ascending to a green hilltop. Little grows
here, in stark contrast to the summit, which appears quite green, and it is a
matter to wonder what past blight has led to the unwholesome nature of this
land.
name: Rising Plain

desc:
What lies before you is nothing short of a wasteland, a wide expanse of dirt,
where even the sturdiest weeds seem unable to gain a foothold. A few ragged and
yellow clumps of dead grass lie, scattered about, long since desiccated by the
cruel wind, which blows across this flat and featureless terrain.
name: Lifeless Plains

desc:
The tall grasses gently sway in the breeze, to create an almost hypnotic,
rustling sound. A few stunted bushes grow here and there, and a lone tree
reaches toward the sky. From here, it is possible to see far across the plains
below.
name: A Green Hilltop

desc:
These devastated plains offer no hope for anything but solitude. Some weeds can
be seen, and here and there a small bush strives for survival. The landscape to
the north and northeast offers no relief from these plains, while to the west
you see a road passing from north to south.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
Little vegetation and much dust makes these plains a desolate place, a sad place
for the life loving, a place of recreation for those of darker persuasions. The
only thing breaking the monotony are stones, pebbles and rocks.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
Absolutely nothing grows here. The pale brown earth is completely bereft of even
weeds or grass, suggestive of a long drought, or perhaps ground so poor in
nutrients that it is quite unable to sustain even the hardiest plantlife.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
A small scattering of sickly plants struggle to grow here, at the side of the
road. Other than that, this place is a stark wasteland, bordered to one side by
the road to Tharbad. To the north, this unappealing wasteland stretches onward,
into the distance.
name: Barren Plains on the Side of a Road

desc:
The walls of this stone building have long since tumbled down and have become
overgrown with grass. It is impossible to discern what purpose this building
originally served, but perhaps it was a small home, or some sort of meeting
hall.
name: Stone Ruins

desc:
Lush vegetation grows on this windy hill top. The view from here is scenic as
you look out to the east. In the far distance, a large stone building can be
seen, and to the south lie the ruins of a small stone building.
name: A Green Hilltop

desc:
From here, parts of the top of the hill can be seen. The hill rises gently, but
the climb is a long one. Reaching the top would be a strenuous exercise. Green
hills climb away to the west and south of here, whilst to the north, an old and
overgrown garden can be seen. All around, the grass grows tall and strong, so
that even the tiny trail heading north is almost lost from sight.
name: Near the Top of a Hill

desc:
This garden must have been very beautiful before it fell into neglect. Now,
weeds have sprouted and begun to strangle flowers and bushes. In the centre of
the garden, it appears there once stood a statue, but it has been knocked over
and is now broken into a few pieces.
name: An Abandoned Garden

desc:
This small cottage might once have been a cosy home, but now it has fallen into
a state of disrepair. The doorway stands empty, the hinges rusted and broken,
and the roof is badly damaged. No furniture remains here but for a few scraps of
wood, scattered about, which are all that is left of the room's former contents.
name: A Small Cottage

desc:
The top of the hill jutts out here a little bit. On either side, the hill still
slopes upward. To the west, some stone ruins can be seen. The wind howls up the
hill and blows quite fiercely here.
name: Atop a Green Hill

desc:
In front of you, to the west, the hilltop jutts out further. However, the hill
is steeper here. In the distance, to the east, you can see a stone fortress on
top of another steep hill.
name: Climbing a Grassy Hill

desc:
The ground slopes quite noticeably, and more grass buds are to be seen here than
on the flatter, broken ground to the east. To the west, lies a long, grassy
hill, which slopes upward toward a plateau.
name: Sloping Grasslands

desc:
The ground here is riddled with stones of various sizes. The earth between the
rocks is a mixture of weeds, dirt and moss. To the west the earth climbs
gradually until it reaches a plateau in the far distance.
name: Broken Ground

desc:
There is an almost discernible evil about this place, for here stands an old
hangman's gallows, its wooden platform worn by age. A rotting noose still swings
back and forth, menacingly, in the slightest breeze. But nowhere is there any
indication who built this, or when.
name: A Hangman's Gallows

desc:
This seems to be a stronghold or a hideout of some kind. It has been hewn out of
a large rock. The floor is paved with large stone slabs that are placed neatly
against each other. From here a few people could defend this place from a large
group of attackers.
name: A Stone Citadel

desc:
The plateau comes to an abrupt end here. To the west, a large stone building can
be seen, at a distance, on the next hill-top, but it seems that it would be
impossible to travel in that direction, as a deep, and impassable gully lies
between the two hills.
name: The Edge of the Plateau

desc:
This hill is a gruelling climb. It leads up to a small stronghold that has been
cut out of a few boulders on top on of the hill. At the foot of the hill is a
small pond with grey water.
name: A Steep Hill

desc:
Large rocks litter this steep incline, but a narrow, winding path seems to pick
its way between the largest boulders. Twisting to the east somewhat, the trail
leads up the hill to a large, wooded plateau.
name: A Rugged Hill

desc:
This valley has a rancid and unwholesome smell. The ground is covered by a
mixture of mud, dead grass and a strange red fungus, which seems to grow
everywhere. To the northwest, a building can be seen atop a large hill.
name: A Decayed Vale among the Hills

desc:
Here lies a small, sickly looking pond. The surface of the water is almost
totally covered with a green slime. The parts of the surface that are not
obscured appear to be grey. Obviously, this would not be the best place to fill
your skins.
name: A Stagnant Pond

desc:
The earth is scarred by an assortment of stones that lay scattered about here.
In among the cracks between the stones grow weeds and moss. This seems to the
end of the plains that go out from here in the east.
name: Broken Grounds

desc:
Huge boulders and dozens of large rocks stand among a littering of smaller
stones, on these flat, and barren plains. It would seem that the blocks of stone
and piles of dust and rubble are unnatural in origin, and their presence here
has choked all life from this field.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
The ground here is quite flat, and littered with so many rocks and stones that
even the crabgrass, so abundant to the west of here, seems unable to gain a
toehold. To the north-east, atop a tall hill, a stone garrison can be seen.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
This field would be ideal farmland, but no sign of intelligent life can be seen
for miles in any direction. An ugly, leafy type of grass grows wild here, coming
up to the ankles of a full grown man.
name: Crabgrass Field

desc:
The hillside is so steep here, that it is all but impossible to go directly down
it without tumbling out of control. Climbing it is sheer torture. To the east is
a plain, which appears to be overgrown with long grasses.
name: A Steep Grassy Incline

desc:
A faint trail through these hilly fields is all but completely hidden by the
long, green grasses, which seem to flourish on this steep hillside. The air here
seems particularly fresh and wholesome, and a cool breeze makes the lush green
blades sway gently.
name: Climbing up a Grassy Hill

desc:
The trail descends down to the floor of the ravine, winding sharply and then
abruptly ending in front of what must be a very thick forest. Even in full
daylight, the forest seems dark and ominous.
name: Trail Descending into a Ravine

desc:
Here the trail goes down for a bit and then rises again and turns east, where it
leads down into the ravine. Looking down into the ravine from here, you can see
only the tops of trees.
name: A Dip in the Trail

desc:
From here, you can look down into a ravine below. The trail is quite narrow, and
it is necessary to proceed with extreme caution to avoid falling down the
cliffside. The trees thin out somewhat, to the north, where the ridge becomes so
narrow as to make passage on horseback seem somewhat risky.
name: A Narrow Trail above a Ravine

desc:
A crude footpath winds through a light forest. The trees seem to thin out to the
north as the trail narrows and seems to go along the top of a ridge. These woods
are suspiciously quiet and there seems to be no sign of intelligent life.
name: A Wooded Trail

desc:
The trees here are so thick that they totally block out the sky and muffle any
noise made. The wind blows the branches, causing a montage of shadows on the
forest floor. It is hard to tell, but it seems that there could be something
moving in the trees ahead.
name: A Wooded Ravine

desc:
It is a rather steep drop down from the road down to this dirt trail, which
continues westward, narrowing markedly as it passes through a tall stand of
trees. The pathway is littered with large numbers of stones of all sizes, which
makes the way somewhat hard to travel.
name: A Trail Down from the Road

desc:
The trees are still dense as they grow right up to the wall of the ravine. This
niche is particularly stagnant due to its isolation. Due to the closeness of the
trees, it is impossible to move in a straight line for more than a few yards in
any direction.
name: A Corner of the Ravine

desc:
This dark forest is reminiscent of descriptions of Mirkwood. There is a
stillness and a sense of forboding here that could chill a person to their very
soul. It is hard to imagine that nature could be evil, but this forest seems
very sinister.
name: A Forest on the Floor of a Ravine

desc:
The valley floor comes to an abrupt end here, as you approach the base of a
cliff. This part of the ravine is less densely wooded than the rest, but even
so, this area remains quite dark and foreboding. Fragments of bone, charred and
splintered pieces of wood, and twisted pieces of metal lie scattered about.
name: End of a Ravine

desc:
This cliff top overlooks a forest at the bottom of a ravine. So dense is the
foliage that the floor of the canyon cannot be seen. What lives below must
remain a matter for speculation, but the woodlands have a sinister air about
them, which hints at all manner of evil.
name: Overlooking a Ravine

desc:
Whoever lives here is not too concerned with cleanliness. Bits of rotten food
are strewn about the floor and the fireplace has not been cleaned for a while.
Even the planks in the walls are warped and cracked due to a lack of upkeep.
name: A Dirty Cabin

desc:
The road upon which you stand seems a well-tended one, bordered, in places, by a
light forest, from which the faint music of bird-songs can be heard. To the
south, the Nen Kalthar makes its way through the countryside, but the bank of
the river is too steep to scramble down here.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road continues its long journey, winding through the countryside on its way
between Bree and Tharbad. To the south, it is bordered by a few trees, growing
in an otherwise featureless flatland, and far in the distance, a small river can
be seen.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road comes to a four-way intersection here. The main route heads south and
west, whilst to the north, there is a small pathway, created by heavy foot
traffic through the grass. Eastward, a small, very stony, dirt path leads away
from the road.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
A small dirt path turns off the road, and leads toward a ramshackle old cabin,
which stands just west of here. Judging by the freshly disturbed soil, this
trail has been used quite recently, and probably quite frequently.
name: A Small Footpath

desc:
An old, dilapidated wooden fence runs east and west here and blocks the way to
the north. Although it is rather run down, it is still too high to climb. The
fence, in all likelihood, is designed to mark a property line, or to keep out
trespassers.
name: An Old Wooden Fence

desc:
An old wooden fence stands here, obviously marking the boundary of somebody's
property. It is too high to be jumped by a horse, and it is also quite clearly
too unstable to be climbed, with planks hanging askew in many places.
name: An Old Wooden Fence

desc:
In this section of this fence, some boards have rotted out and someone has made
a hole large enough for one person on horseback to pass through. To the east,
the fence ends and the grounds becomes more rocky. Through the hole you can see
a vast expanse of plains before a hill.
name: A Hole in the Fence

desc:
Nothing grows here anymore. Clumps of dead grass are everywhere here. For some
reason the ground is not as fertile as the green land a few miles to the south.
To the north, there is a hill with some sort of stone structure on top of it.
name: Dead Plains

desc:
Lush amber grain grows across these fields, which are bordered, to the north, by
a large wooden fence. To the south, the rich golden meadow continues, whilst
east of here lie uncultivated fields.
name: Golden Plains

desc:
All around grows lush grain of a rich and vivid shade of gold. Plump ears grow
atop long, slender stalks, which dance in the slightest breeze, making this look
like an amber sea, which stretches into the distance, to the north. To the west
lies a small pond, and south of here is a small, stony lane.
name: Golden Plains

desc:
This small pond is ideal for fishing. It is crystal clear and beautiful. Its
surface is placid and calm. Around the water's edge grow lovely tufts of green
grass. To the east a vast expanse of golden grain opens out before you.
name: A Clear Pond

desc:
A thin layer of small stones and gravel would make this a good path to tread,
even in wet weather. However, a number of larger rocks, scattered here and
there, could easily trip an incautious traveller. To the west the lane makes an
intersection with the road to Tharbad, whilst to the north-east a small building
can be seen.
name: A Small Stony Lane

desc:
The road upon which you stand seems to be both well-tended and much-travelled.
It is bordered to the west by an obviously ancient woodland, made up of a number
of stout oaks. From somewhere in that direction, the sound of bird-song can be
heard.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road makes its way south and west from here, as it journeys between Bree and
Tharbad. The dirt and stones are hard-packed on this thoroughfare, a clear
indication of the heavy use it has seen over the years, and no grass or weeds
blemish its surface.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The rustic lane heads out of the farm yard to the east and leads to the road to
Tharbad in the distant west. On the north side of the lane, there is a lush
field full of golden grain.
name: A Small Stony Lane

desc:
This small field seems to overflow with healthy, green grass, making it a
perfect place for livestock to graze. It is bounded on two sides by a neat
wooden fence, separating it from the road to Tharbad, to the west, and from a
small farm lane, to the north.
name: A Green Pasture

desc:
Patches of wild grass and dirt are abundant in this run-farm yard, and the
hedgerow to the east grows wild and unkempt. To the immediate north stands an
old farmhouse, which appears to be in dire need of repairs, whilst south of here
lies a green pasture.
name: A Farm Yard

desc:
The foliage here grows wild and thick, and it almost seems that there is no way
out of this undergrowth. To the north, south and west, the branches seem to be
so tangled that no progress is possible.
name: Among the Hedgerow

desc:
Tall, lush grasses cover these gentle, rolling hills, swaying in the lightest
breeze, as if some small animal were moving through the dark blades. To the
north stands a large wooden fence, whilst westward the flatlands continue.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
These plains roll gently, but for the most part, are fairly flat. Perhaps these
fields belong to a farmer who uses them as pasture for his livestock. To the
north, these fields are bordered by a large wooden fence, which seems to stretch
endlessly, both to the east, and to the west.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
Lush green grass grows strong and healthy in this lovely pasture, which would be
an ideal spot for grazing cattle, sheep or horses. The fields extend to the
south, east and west of here, whilst to the north lies a small, slightly unkempt
farmyard.
name: A Fertile Pasture

desc:
Clearly this portion of the pastureland has been badly overgrazed, with all of
the grass bitten down to the ground, to leave only bare dirt. The endless
passage of animals over this field has left it a quagmire.
name: A Muddy Cow Pasture

desc:
A few bales of hay are stacked in one corner of this ample building, and on the
west wall a neat row of stalls has been built for the animals. In the north
wall, a large opening lets out onto a muddy pasture.
name: Inside a Large Barn

desc:
Thick grass grows in abundance here, and trees stand at intervals over the
field, with small clusters of flowers growing about their bases. You can catch a
glimpse of a small house to the north, and just east of here stands an old
wooden barn.
name: A Grazing Pasture

desc:
This well grown grassland is located in a bend of the Road to Tharbad, which
lies to the west and to the south of here. The fields are filled with thick and
high grass, and a few small bushes grow here and there, punctuating the meadow,
which stretches onward to the north and east.
name: A Grassland beside the Road

desc:
The bare hard soil of the road to Tharbad looks like a brown cut in the middle
of the green grassy plains. The ground has many slashes in it from where
merchant carriages once rode. Grass grows fairly tall within the neglected
plains nearby, creating many homes for insects.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road to Tharbad winds from the north along to the east, and is bordered by
the bank of a river on the southern and western side. The embankment slopes down
fairly steeply, with soft red clay marking the surface of the bank. Around here
and towards the north there is nothing except more fields and some burnt out
carriages along the road.
name: Bend in the Road

desc:
The road extends along the flat barren land towards the east and in the west.
The hard dirt road winds and curves along the terrain like a snake moving in the
desert. A small embankment slopes towards the south, leading to a fast flowing
river. The road itself does not offer much to look at.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
Carriage tracks scar the ground in many different directions, making deep
slicing gouges in the hardened surface of the road. Beside the lane, tall grass
grows in dense clumps, which lean in the light breeze, and to the south, beyond
the continuing roadway, the steep banks of the Nen Kalthar can be seen.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
Tall grass grows all over within this field, unlike the hard and bare land of
the nearby road. Small holes lie here and there, in the ground, home to
innumerable small beasts, and to the north, a large wooden building can be seen,
hinting at the presence of a nearby farm.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
The edges of the road slope gently away, making its border that much more
clearly defined. The nearby fields look unattractive and muddy, in contrast to
the hard-packed dirt of the thoroughfare. A few trees grow beside the roadway,
not too far distant.
name: Bend in the Road

desc:
This stretch of wooded plains is bounded to the north and east by the road to
Tharbad. To the west, it comes to an end on the banks of the Nen Kalthar. Here
stands a small wood, comprised of obviously ancient oak trees, whose gnarled
roots stand proud of the ground in places.
name: Wooded Plain

desc:
A featureless grassy field stretches beside the roadway, and nearby the Nen
Kalthar flows peacefully through the countryside. The ground here is moist and
cool, the grass long and green, its roots fed by the water of the river.
However, the road to the east is made of hard-packed earth, which somehow seems
parched even in wet weather.
name: Small Grassy Field

desc:
A tall and stately tree stands atop a steep river bank. Though quite ancient the
tree is healthy and strong, and its base is surrounded by thick, green grass.
The soft sound of the river babbling below lends a special music to the scene.
name: A Large Tree beside the River

desc:
The riverbanks here are too steep to be scaled. Trees along each bank sway
gently in the slightest breeze, dropping leaves into the gently rippling water,
which laps against the land as it progresses south.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The water slowly rolls across the land and takes a turn to the south here. To
the east, a series of rolling plains can be seen. To the north, a road runs
parallel to the river, marching off to the east, as the river bends southward.
name: A Calm Bend in the Nen Kalthar

desc:
The cool water of the Nen Kalthar can be seen from atop the river bank. The path
down to the water is steep and treacherous, as the shoreline is quite rocky, and
slick with water, and to the east the road the Tharbad stretches into the
distance, cutting a long scar through the countryside.
name: River Bank

desc:
The riverbank to the north is too steep to scale. Lazily the current moves
downstream and ripples as it passes a large rock, which stands right in the
middle of the waterway, its smooth surface moist and slightly mossy.
name: Rock in the Nen Kalthar

desc:
Large, slightly slippery, rocks peep out above the surface of the water, which
ripples and foams around them. The shallow shoreline is filled with tiny,
multicoloured pebbles, and beneath the shimmering water, a riverbed of small
rocks can be seen.
name: Fording the Nen Kalthar

desc:
This area is covered with thick green grass. The grass is well grown becasue of
its never failing access to water. It is a sand bank to the east, which is
bordering the Nen Kalthar, the sand shines with a soft yellow light. The field
reaches further to the west as far as you can see.
name: On a Grassy Bank

desc:
Nature demonstrates her power here, in this green billowing sea of grass. The
field stretches eastward and to the south, amid the tall grasses, stands a lone
tree, watching over the surrounding countryside.
name: In the Thick Grass

desc:
A sad and lonely looking tree stands here, amid the tall grass, its branches
stripped almost bare by the hard winds which blow so often over these open
plains. The few remaining leaves have a sickly brown look, as if scarcely
clinging to life. Around the tree stand a few short bushes, with dense foliage,
better able to withstand the harsh winds.
name: At a Lonely Tree

desc:
The serenity of this small river is remarkable. The clear waters gently flow
onward, to the east, as they pass through a small grove of trees. In the
distance, an old road can be seen, running alongside the river, on its endless
journey through the surrounding country.
name: The Majestic Nen Kalthar

desc:
Of all the rivers on Arda, this one must be the most picturesque. The trickling
it makes as it flows is music to a weary person's ears. The Nen Kalthar bends
and flows to the east here.
name: A Shallow Bend in the Nen Kalthar

desc:
A few trees grow along the road here. To the west and the south, the trees end
as one gets close to the bank of the Nen Kalthar. To the north and the east, the
road can be seen through the trees.
name: Scattered Woods along the Road

desc:
You wander amid a tranquil, grassy plain, made fertile by the small river, which
flows just north and east of here. The plain reaches, unbound, to the horizon,
like an endless sea of waving green grass, with small wildflowers and numerous
thick bushes to be seen among the greenery.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The grass here is long, and grows with a healthy green lustre. These plains,
which stretch to the east and west, as far as the eye can see, have been made
fertile, by their proximity to the small river, which flows eastward across the
land, just north of here.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
These plains are rich and green, owing much of their health to the rich water
supply from the small river just north of here. The plains stretch out to the
horizon both east and west. The river is small, but looks rather deep, so it
would be difficult to cross here.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The plain is lush and green, the grass rich and healthy. The babble of a small
river to the north, and the sweet sound of birdsong make music in the air,
unbroken except by an occasional gust of wind, which rolls across these endless
flatlands.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The grassy plain unfolds before you, rich and lush with vegetation. Green grass
covers your ankles as you stride along. The plain stretches as far as you can
see both east and west, although you glimpse rising treetops to the west. A
river cleaves the plain to the north, running east and west. Although not too
wide, it seems rather deep, so crossing here could be difficult.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The plains look rich and fertile here, propably owing a lot of its health to the
rich watersupply from the river to the north. The plain stretches as far as you
can see along the river to the east, while the view west is obscured by the
growing vegetation and the rising trees of a small forest.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The vegetation is dense here, but not tall enough to be called a forest. Yet it
grows tight, and you have to force your way through here. Looking west, you see
the trees growing larger and larger, while to the east it thins down to a grassy
plain.
name: Brush

desc:
Some large trees among the thick undergrowth makes this place more or less a
forest. Yet passage is slow, the bushes whipping at your face as you pass. The
vegetation grows denser to the west. Somewhere to the north you hear the
tinkling of a river.
name: Overgrown Forest

desc:
An ancient oak stands, quite alone, at the centre of a circle of smaller trees.
Far past its prime, this enormous tree shows signs of rot, and many termites
have consumed and eroded its once sturdy trunk. However, it still stands, its
broad branches making a wide canopy above your head.
name: Overgrown Forest

desc:
You find yourself atop the tallest tree in the forest, with a panoramic view of
the surrounding countryside. Beyond the forest to the north you see a river and
a road side by side, leading eastwards. Further on you see a flat and rather
large plain unfolding, the flatness broken by a hill and some buildings.
name: Up in the Oak

desc:
The river is quite deep here, but its waters are clear, and a number of small
shapes can be seen, darting about above the stony bed. To the south, a wide
grassy plain stretches into the distance, whilst to the north of the river, a
wide roadway marches from east to west.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The river is wide and quite shallow here, with numerous broad, flat rocks,
peeping above the surface of the slow-flowing water. Grassy plains fringe the
south bank, stretching off into the distance, whilst to the north, three
roadways meet.
name: A Shallow Part of the Nen Kalthar

desc:
The river is very deep here, but not at all wide. Through the clear water, small
glimmering shapes can be seen darting hither and thither. The flow here is quite
serene, progressing gently toward the east, whilst beyond the banks of the
river, to the north, a road can be seen, tracing a similar route.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The river is deeper here than it seemed, flowing slowly eastward. Small
glimmering shapes down in the river scatters as you pass. A road seems to follow
the river to the north, while a grassy plain unfolds to the south.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
Flowing west and east, the river is here too deep to cross without swimming.
Just north you see a road following the river, while grassy plains can be found
to the south. Gazing into the depths of the water, you glimpse small shapes,
darting around.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The river is quite deep here, but some large flat rocks, only partially covered
by moss and weeds, provide a fairly safe footing. The Nen Kalthar bends east and
north here, and is bordered to the south by gently rolling plains. To the
northeast, a steep cliff rises out of the water, and peering downwards, it can
be seen that this stretches far below the surface, even though it only rises
some four feet above the waterline.
name: On a Flat Rock

desc:
The stream bends west and south here, counterpointing the road to the north.
Although not wide, the river seems to be quite deep here. You see twinkles of
light reflected in small, darting shapes near the bottom of the river.
name: A Bend in the River

desc:
The river is deep and clear here, in fact much deeper than it seemed from afar.
Looking down, you see glimmering shapes by the stony bottom, quickly darting
here and there. To the north a small road follows the river, while to the
southeast you see some trees.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The river is very shallow here, propably because much of the water has found its
way into the marsh to the north. To the south small trees can be seen, growing
taller further south and west. The river gets deeper further east.
name: A Shallow Part of the Nen Kalthar

desc:
You stand in the middle of a rather shallow river. Its hard to say where the
river ends and the marsh to the north begins, since much of the water flows
freely there. The cluttered forest to the south seems comfortable compared to
the soggy mire to the north.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The mud is sticky and deep, and the air alive with the buzzing of countless
small insects, most of which seem intent on making you their dinner. A faint
rotting smell assails your nostrils, and even the ferns and small, stunted
bushes seem to struggle to grow here.
name: Marsh

desc:
The ground is slightly elevated here, making the soil dry. No ferns or trees
obscure the ground, making this place a haven in this mire. Even the buzzing of
the insects have faded.
name: Marsh

desc:
This wet mire dooms any boot passing through to instant sogginess. The marsh is
deep and dense enough to force anyone to struggle to get through, and a few
small animal bones peep up through the surface of the foetid mud. The air all
around is filled, not only by a rotting stench, but also by the insistent buzz
of innumerable insects.
name: Marsh

desc:
Stunted bushes and large ferns occupy any traces of dry land in this soggy mire,
leaving any attempt to pass through the area without getting soaked assured to
fail. All around, the air is filled by the insistent buzzing of innumerable
small insects.
name: Marsh

desc:
The air seems alive with small insects, which buzz incessantly about you, and is
filled too with a faint rotting stench. Dark green ferns and small, stunted
bushes cling to life in the quagmire, occupying any small island of dryness in
the mud, and forcing any traveller to move through the deepest parts of the
mire.
name: Marsh

desc:
The river gently separates the forest to the south from the mire to the north.
Where the river becomes mire is hard to tell, but crossing the river will cause
no problem. The river is shallow, and flows serenely from east to west.
name: Small River

desc:
Brambles and briars grow thick among the trees, and to the south, they grow so
densely as to obscure the trail completely, making progress that way quite
impossible. To the west, too, the undergrowth is choking and no clear pathway
can be seen. The soil here is moist, and the air thick with a faint musty odour,
and from somewhere to the north, the sound of a river can be heard.
name: Overgrown Forest

desc:
Even though you stand in the outskirts of the forest, the trees grow close
together here, and a thick underbrush of briars and brambles makes progress
through this area quite difficult. An occasional howl pierces the air, obscuring
the tranquil sound of flowing water, somewhere to the north.
name: Overgrown Forest

desc:
You force your way through this brush. Small, intertwined trees and bushes makes
the passage very tiresome. The tops of larger trees can be seen to the south and
west, while to the north a small river passes eastward.
name: Brush

desc:
Small trees and dense bushes are entwined to form what can only be called a
brush. The trees are too small to be a true forest, but seem to be striving to
grow into one. To the southwest the top of taller trees can be seen.
name: Brush

desc:
The air around you is damp and foul, filled with a faint rotting stench, and all
about the incessant buzzing of countless small insects can be heard. Far to the
south, beyond a small river, a forest can be seen, but to the north, east and
west the marshland stretches into the distance.
name: Marsh

desc:
The ground seems firmer here, the mire less evident. You find yourself before an
ancient willow, worn by rot and age, and cleft in half by some wild strike of a
lightning bolt long ago. A loud buzzing is heard, louder the closer you get to
the willow.
name: Rotten Tree

desc:
You are on an ancient road, small of stature, yet with a dignity born from
ancient use. Your passage disturbs some weeds breaking through the hard-packed
ground. The road leads in a straight east to west direction, but the way
westward has been blocked by the fallen bough of a tree.
name: Old Road

desc:
The light from the entrance to the south is strangely magnified here, perhaps
due to multitudes of crystals, embedded in the walls, which reflect and enhance
the light, making it possible to discern some of the details in this cave.
Searching around, you find little of interest, not even a pocket of air. The
only thing worth a closer look are some boulders and rocks blocking the way
further north. Now and then something unknown touches your feet fleetingly.
name: Watery Cave

desc:
You are diving into the river. Small fish scatter from your prescence as you
explore the watery depths. The river is unexpectedly deep here, and due to the
turbulent and muddy water, your vision is limited. At the southern edge of the
river, you see an uneven cliff plunging into the depths. The cliff has many
rather wide cracks in it, some of which seems to be human-sized too.
name: Underwater

desc:
You stand on what seems to be the highest point in the area. Turning slowly, you
see that if nothing else, this is a place of contrasts. To the north you see
mostly desolated fields and some ruined buildings. To the south you see a river
flowing eastward along a road, while far to the southwest you see a small
forest. Just southeast is the junction of three roads, Greenway, the Road to
Tharbad, and a less used road leading towards Sarn Ford.
name: A Small Hill

desc:
Unwholesome plains surrounds you. The soil is cluttered with stones and rocks,
yet a little greenery can still be seen, though most of this is merely weeds and
a little choked, unhealthy grass. The plains ascend a little to the east, as
they climb a small hillock.
name: Desolate Plains

desc:
These dreary plains offer little solace for a weary traveller. Rocks of various
sizes, from pebbles to large boulders, are found in multitudes here. Just east
you see a large road cutting through the plains, running straight north.
name: Desolate Plains

desc:
These dry and desolate plains offer little peace for a traveller. Rocks and
weeds stain the earth. The soil is cracked and hard, the weeds are obviously the
only thing able to survive.
name: Desolate Plains

desc:
These plains are desolated, in dire need of vegetation. The ground is rather
flat, but, here and there, small crevices in the earth can be seen. An
occasional boulder stands tall, defiant in the face of erosion.
name: Desolate Plains

desc:
These fields were once rich, yet now only the weeds survive. A faint rotten
smell permeates this area. The soil is hard and cracked. An abandoned building
can be seen to the northeast, torn by age and weather.
name: Dry Fields

desc:
The furrows and the rotten remains of fences are all that remains of these
fields of old. Whatever crop was growing here, it has now been replaced by
weeds. Just north you see a partly collapsed building.
name: Dry Fields

desc:
This building is partly collapsed, the fireplace by one wall now nothing but
loose stones and bricks from the collapsed chimney. Evil thoughts and evil deeds
seems strangely manifest in the cold air. A door leads out east, with a moldy
rug lying in front of it.
name: Ruined Inn

desc:
Rotten fences have long ago crumbled, yet still outline what once must have been
a crop field. The weeds have long since replaced the crops, being able to
survive in these inhospitable surroundings. The remains of a ruined building can
be seen to the east.
name: Dry Fields

desc:
The soil here seems nutritious, at least when compared to the land further west,
where the grasses grow yellow and weak. To the east, a small building stands in
the distance, beyond a wide open field, and in the far distance, a roadway can
be seen.
name: Plains

desc:
The weeds seem dominant here, yet large tuffs of grass sprout here and there,
giving this region a ragged appearance. The ground is uneven, rising and
sinking, occasionally breaking into small clefts in the landscape.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The plain is flat, the grass a sickly pale green colour. There is little to
attract anyone here, save perhaps some starved animal. Just east of here you see
a faint path, partly overgrown, yet obviously leading north to a small hill.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
Green grass competes with weeds for survival on these plains. A sense of despair
is somehow present here, tugging at your awareness, yet somehow beyond your
perceptions. To the northwest, the plains rises to a small hill.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
A nauseating stench fills the air. The grass grows high, but it has a faint
yellowish tinge to it, breaking the illusion of health. A partly overgrown path
cleaves through the grassland, leading north to a tiny hill. The feeble
chittering of grasshoppers is the only sound to be heard.
name: Faint Path

desc:
One side of the barn has collapsed long ago, and what remains looks rather
unstable too. Moving around here requires care. All you seem to find here are
the yellowed bones of a cow-sized animal.
name: Collapsed Barn

desc:
Only a pale shadow of the activity of old remains here now. Once neat, the
furrows which line the ground are now choked by intruding weeds. A crumbling
fence runs along the road to the east, and also stands between this field and
the plains, which lie to the south.
name: Fields

desc:
An old, stony trail, laid to waste by ages of disuse, and exposure to the wind
and weather, leads westward, toward a ruined farm. Crumbling fences border the
trail, outlining ancient fields, to the north and south, whilst the pathway
itself, leads east, to the Greenway.
name: Old Trail

desc:
Only a pale memory remains of these once rich and fertile pastures. The grass
here seems to be fighting a losing battle against the encroaching weeds, and the
fence, which once bordered these fields on three sides, now lies in ruins to the
north, leaving the way open to a blighted and desolate plain. To the east, the
fence, though decayed still bars the way to a wide road, which carves a bold
north to south route through the countryside.
name: Fields

desc:
The interior of the farmhouse is a complete mess. A thick layer of grime coats
the floor, and the broken remains of the furniture lie scattered all around,
themselves blanketed in a thick covering of dust and cobwebs. It would appear
that this place was abandoned long ago.
name: Decayed Farm House

desc:
The farmyard is covered by flagstones, crumbled by age and the weeds which have
forced their way up to the sun. A dry stone wall marks the margin of the land to
the west, and a small pig pen, outlined by a rotting wooden fence, stands just
before the barn to the south. To the north stands an old farm house.
name: Farmyard

desc:
Though the grass grows quite tall here, it has an unhealthy, yellow hue. A few
small bushes seem barely able to cling to life here, and the overall feeling of
the area is one of decay and disease. These sickly plains stretch onward to the
north and south, but to the west, a small hill rises out of the flat landscape.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The yellow grass grows about ankle-high here, and a faint, pungent, rotting
smell permeates the air. Though a few small flowers strive to survive, they seem
to face a hopeless battle in this obviously unhealthy region. All that can be
seen to the north and the south is a continuing expanse of barren plains, much
like these.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
Standing alone in the middle of the grassy plains, this hill provides a decent
place to scout the surrounding area. Covered by grass, this hill is crowned by a
large cluster of bushes, cradling a rather unusual indentation in the side of
the hill.
name: Tiny Hill

desc:
This cellar shows signs of decades of abandonment. A thick layer of dust covers
the room like a shroud. Some of the walls have crumbled with time, showing the
bare earth behind. Judging from the broken barrels and rotten remains hanging
from the ceiling, this must once have been a food store.
name: Dusty Cellar

desc:
As you entered the abandoned inn, a portion of the floor gave way underfoot,
plunging you into this dark cellar. The air has a musty and rotten odour,
seemingly quite unhealthy to breath, and the ground is covered with broken
shards of bone, which crumble beneath your feet.
name: Ruined Inn

desc:
The current of the water is moving slowly, though, heading down south, it
appears to be picking up some speed. Rocks can be seen on the bottom of the
river, they are becoming more and more noticeable drifting downstream. The
river's course heads south, and also leads back towards the north.
name: Drifting down the Nen Kalthar

desc:
The stream flows peacefully here, the gentle current creating multitudinous
ripples and eddies on the surface of the water. Sand covers the entire bed of
the river, and piles upward at the banks as well, left by the flow of the water.
Every so often a light breeze blows across the surface of the water, making
criss-cross patterns in the ripples.
name: Sandy River

desc:
More rocks accumulate towards the east, making the river begin to roar loudly.
Further upstream, to the west, the stream widens, and the banks become quite
sandy, the waters more murky, and the current more languid. But here, the grassy
banks are covered with trees and bushes, whose leaves fall into the water, and
drift downstream.
name: Calm Riverbed

desc:
Two different waterways meet here, a small stream, flowing from the west,
joining the Nen Kalthar in its eastward flow here. The current picks up pace,
becoming swifter to the east, where more rocks can be seen, peaking through the
surface of the water. Westward, the stream is more peaceful, with a sandy
bottom, which swirls up, making the waters murky, as small fish swim about,
raising the sand.
name: Bend in the River

desc:
The current gently moves the water towards the east, softly stirring up the
sandy bottom. There is a greenish tint to the surface of the stream, which
obscures the true depth, but a few old branches poke through the water, creating
small waves, and suggesting that perhaps this waterway is quite shallow.
name: Bend in the River

desc:
Slowly and quietly the stream drifts onward to the east. Tiny tracks in the soft
mud of the shoreline hint at the presence of small unseen creatures, and faint
noises interrupt the tranquility of the scene. Trees, standing on the shore, bow
down, dipping their branches toward the water, in a gentle breeze, and the
blue-green water laps in small waves, toward the banks of the river.
name: Calm River

desc:
The riverbank has worn away to reveal this small, mossy niche. The ground is
soft, earthy and moist, and the curving walls on all sides are covered in a
thick blanket of dark green moss. The sound of the river to the north is all
that is to be heard.
name: A Hole in the Riverbank

desc:
White foam forms all over the rocks as the current becomes more and more rapid.
The water is cool as it quickly drifts by, with the air moving across the top of
the surface. Very small rocks along the beach of the river swirl the water into
tiny little whirlpools.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The swift waters of the river are becoming more and more rough as the current
heads off to slash the water against rocks breaking the surface. Mist fills this
place as the water is splashed high into the air when it collides with the
rocks.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The river current has become swift and rough, slashing out over the rocks that
break the water's surface. Creating a loud roar, which masks any sounds coming
anywhere away from there, the river quickly and violently rolls on towards the
east, with many white bubbles forming on its surface.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The rocks in the river are becoming rounder in shape, breaking the water over
them much softer than before. The sound of rough water from up the river is
still extremely loud, and a reminder of how swift and treacherous it once was. A
crisp, cool breeze waves over the surface of the water, chilling it slightly.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
Many old and burnt out carriages lie scattered along the roadside, which
meanders from the east and towards the west. The ground underfoot is hard and
bare, from the wear of many years of travel.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The bank of the river bends along it, towards the east and in the north. The
ground is very moist from the water splashing against the rocks and landing on
the soil above. Many rocks can be seen breaking the surface of the river,
creating little side currents within the water.
name: River Bank

desc:
Up along the bank, the water appears to have a blue greenish tint to it. The
river bends around the bank, and a large tree stands over the river, its leaves
slightly dipping into the water from their drooping branches. A few brightly
coloured leaves float softly into the mainstream current, getting transported
briskly down along the river.
name: River Bank

desc:
The rocky riverbank peters out to the east, where the road to Tharbad draws
closer to the waters of the Nen Kalthar. Large, flat slabs of rock rise steeply
upward from the rippling water, too steep and slick to climb; most seem flat and
featureless, but one particularly large slab seems oddly scratched and notched.
name: River Bank

desc:
The water here has become so shallow that it is possible for even someone the
size of a halfling to wade through it. The bed of the river has very few rocks
that lie on the bottom here, exposing a muddy surface that appears to have been
disturbed many times. Wind dances along the top of the water creating ripples on
the surface.
name: Shallow Part of the River

desc:
The field slopes gently down toward the river, and is covered by waves of lush
green grass. Here and there, clusters of beautifully coloured flowers can be
seen, scattered among the grass, their heads bobbing in the light breeze.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
A few green berry bushes are growing here, and there is a pattern to their
growth that aligns with a nearby white fence. The stems of the bushes have
small, sharp thorns on them, becoming a minor annoyance to walk through. An old
wooden fence borders the patch on three sides, leaving it open only to the east.
However several of its planks seem to be quite loose.
name: Berry Patch

desc:
Thick vegetation fills this area, which is bounded to the north by an old wooden
fence. To the south and the east, the vegetation is so thick that it is
impossible to force a way through the thorny bushes, but to the west, it might
be possible to find a way deeper into the woodland.
name: In the Outskirts of the Forest

desc:
There are thick roots and vines spread all over the ground, which makes it
almost impossible to pass through this area, and tall trees form a canopy
overhead. Large bushes and shrubs choke the whole area, and it is quite
impossible to tell what lies very far ahead.
name: Thick Undergrowth

desc:
Wheat grows in abundance in this field, some of which seems ready to be
harvested. A fence borders the fields, with berry bushes climbing up the fence
in the west. Another field, filled with corn, stands in the north, with a small
house nearby.
name: Wheat Field

desc:
Like the wheat field, there is nothing more than a field of corn. A tall
scarecrow stands in the middle of the field, fending off crows to no avail. Much
of the corn in the field has been eaten, or knocked onto the ground. Corn stalks
lie everywhere around the ground, and along the fence. To the north, the
babbling river can be heard.
name: Corn Field

desc:
Some hay is piled up against the fence, covering part of it from view. The
ground here is muddy and there are many hoof prints embedded in it. A sound of
clanking bells can be heard coming from somewhere around here. The farm house
can be easily seen now, as smoke rises from a small chimney atop the slate roof.
name: Cow Pasture

desc:
Smoke can be seen curling up from the chimney atop a farm house, slowly drifting
over and across the top of a large barn nearby. A small path leads back through
the fields, toward the river, with tall grass marking the boundary of the trail.
The wind blows across the grassy field making it look like an endless sea on
land.
name: Field before a House

desc:
This well worn trail runs through an endless field of grass, stretching off into
the distance. To the north you can see a large pile of grey, moss- covered
rocks, which block the route to the Nen Kalthar.
name: A Trail through the Fields

desc:
Lush grasses grow tall throughout the area, and a few small trees stand to the
west, overshadowing some small bushes. The soft, almost musical sound of the
river hangs in the air, accompanying the freshness of the water. To the south,
the rooftop of a small house can be spied.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
A tiny square window is set above an old kitchen sink along one wall of the
room. The kitchen cabinets end in the back corner of the house, where a large
box, filled with firewood, stands. A large fireplace dominates the middle of the
room, its hot coals spewing smoke, which rises and curls, seeming to caress an
antique sword, hanging high above the mantle. Small chairs, with seats woven of
straw, stand in the centre of the floor.
name: Farmer's House

desc:
Stalls line the sides of the barn, with a huge loft looming overhead. Bales of
hay lie stacked in lines and rows along the edge of the loft up above. The
smells of a farm are very evident here, with many different animal droppings
littering the ground. An old lantern hangs from a nail in a rail overhead, at
about the right height for a human farmer.
name: Large Red Barn

desc:
This stony farmyard must once have been quite pretty, with borders of rose
bushes set along the fence to the south. But now all is overgrown and the red
painted boards of the house, and its white eaves are now chipped and peeling.
One window stands broken in its frame. To the north lies the bank of the Nen
Kalthar, whilst a stony pathway leads eastward.
name: An Old Farmyard

desc:
This is a well grown sandy bank which is bordering the Nen Kalthar. There are
some small bushes on one side of the bank, they are green with thick leaves and
they have some small red berries on them. Further up on the bank there is nice
soft green grass, a perfect spot to lay down and feel the air from the river.
name: On the Bank of the Nen Kalthar

desc:
A small field lies fallow before an old barn, bordered on the south and west by
a rotting wooden fence. The grass underfoot has an unwholesome appearance, which
has probably resulted from the encroaching trees of the ancient woodland, which
stands to the south. There lies a dense and unpassable underbrush, above which
the massive trees loom.
name: Field before a Barn

desc:
The boards in the attic creak heavily with each step taken up the stairs. The
attic has very little light coming in from its one small window. There are many
boxes piled up in the corner of the attic, and a few small rats can be heard
squeaking around in the darkness. The air smells of the staleness this room has
to offer.
name: Attic

desc:
A large cavern lies hidden here, hacked painstakingly out of the solid rock
which makes up the riverbank. The digging was obviously done with little
sophistication or skill, for the walls and ceiling are rough and unfinished, and
only the floor is smooth. The dim light of a torch flickers from a high recess
in one of the walls.
name: Alcove of Smugglers

desc:
Though the entrance to this hole was rather small, the cave opens up somewhat,
further inside. The roof hangs low above your head, forcing anyone of great
stature to stoop uncomfortably. A crunching sound underfoot gives pause for
thought; the entire floor of this chamber is covered by splinters of bone, most
of which seems to have been from small animals.
name: Dry Hole

desc:
Though it is almost vertical in places, there seems to be no difficulty in
climbing up or down this narrow, sloping tunnel, as there are many large rocky
outcroppings, which almost seem like a stair. The ceiling above is beaded with
small droplets of water, which drip all around, creating a quiet splash as they
hit the stony floor.
name: A Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The cavern creaks with the pressure of the river above, and the ceiling bulges
downward, as if barely able to support its own weight. Water pours in from
above, running down the walls to add to the flow in this tunnel, and the rock
all around has been worn quite smooth by its unending flow.
name: Underground Stream

desc:
Cold water seeps in on all sides, adding to the eastward flow, which continues
its way through a small opening in the rock wall. To the north lies a stony
landing, whilst to the west, the stream stretches onward into the darkness.
name: Underground Stream

desc:
Water floods into the cavern from the river above, but the sloping floor causes
rapid drainage, into the beginning of an underground stream, to the west. Large
stalactites hang down from the ceiling, reaching to the floor in places, like
massive columns, seeming to support the cave. The walls to each side are pitted
with innumerable tiny holes, which might be the home to small creatures of the
darkness, and animal bones lie scattered about the floor.
name: Watery Tunnel

desc:
No sound is to be heard, except for the insistent flow of the river above. The
very presence of this sound is unsettling, and the many cracks and fissures in
the roof of this small chamber, through which water trickles and drips, can only
add to the impression that one day the river will break through, and flood this
area.
name: A Small Landing

desc:
This clear stream is beautiful. The water moves gently downstream. Despite its
coolness, the water still seems inviting. The Nen Kalthar bends and flows south
from here.
name: Nen Kalthar

desc:
Straight as an arrow, the roadway continues its long journey north-south between
Bree and Tharbad. The ground underfoot is hard and gouged with the deep ruts
caused by the passage of many heavily-laden wagons over the years. Vast dry
plains extend on either side of the road.
name: Greenway

desc:
Ever onward, the wide road continues its north-south march between Bree and
Tharbad. Here it crosses a vast area of open plains, characterised by a covering
of insipid grasses. Though packed down to be almost as hard as rock, the dirt
which comprises the surface of the road nevertheless bears the marks of the
heavy use it has borne over the years since it was built.
name: Greenway

desc:
A few straggly patches of thin, dry yellow grass grow here and there in the
parched soil. To the north, the ground looks more fertile, the grass greener and
there is some light vegetation, but here the overall impression is one of an
unnatural pallor. The grass seems to almost die out to the west, while to the
east it seems there is a road.
name: Dry Plain

desc:
The plain is arid and barren. Little vegetation is seen, except some short
grass. Rocks of various sizes decorate the plain. A road runs north-south in the
distance to the west. The dry plain continues in all other directions.
name: Dry Plain

desc:
The plain is dry and desolate. There is little vegetation to be seen. The ground
seems inhospitable to any plant life. Stones and rocks are abundant. A similar
landscape extends in every direction. There is a hushed soundscape, with the
occasional soft skittering of a lizard's footsteps, the gentle sighs of the wind
as it weaves its way through the barren expanse, and the occasional distant
rattle of loose stones tumbling in the stillness.
name: Dry Plain

desc:
The arid plain is devoid of vegetation. Rocks of various shapes and sizes are
scattered across the landscape. A deep gulch runs along its northern boundary,
making northward journeys hazardous. Some bushes grow at the edges of the gulch.
name: Dry Plain

desc:
The land all around seems dry and desolate, with little vegetation maintaining a
foothold in the parched soil. Rocks and stones of various sizes lie all about,
scattered among the thin and sickly tufts of grass, but there is little else to
be seen. To the north, a deep cleft cuts across the landscape, where once a
rapid river flowed.
name: Dry Plain

desc:
The plain extends in all directions, yet the grass appears drier and less
verdant compared to the western regions. Moving east, the vegetation becomes
increasingly scarce, and notably, the usual sounds of nature seem notably absent
in this region.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
The grass is almost non-existent here. The soil seems dry, almost like dust. A
little stream of water descends from the south, from the side of a small hill.
It trickles into a little pond, barely nine feet across. The plain extends to
the north, east and west.
name: Pond in the Plain

desc:
The plains here exude lushness and vitality. Natural sounds animate the air,
contributing to the vibrant atmosphere. The grass stands tall and verdant,
painting the landscape with its rich green hue. A diverse array of flowers
thrives in this fertile environment, while small bushes and shrubs proliferate
throughout the area.
name: Lush and Grassy Plain

desc:
Delicate flowers of every hue flourish among the tall, verdant grasses of this
fertile plain. Abundant small bushes and shrubs provide habitats for numerous
tiny creatures, whose constant chatter fills the air. Far to the east, the
Greenway cuts across the plain.
name: Flowering Plain

desc:
The plain here is teeming with vitality. The air is filled with the sound of
chirping and the bustling activity of numerous tiny creatures. Abundant flowers
adorn the plain, flourishing amidst the luxuriant, verdant grass. The expansive
plain stretches endlessly into the distance.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
Though the grass is quite green, it is spread sparsely, among a scattering of
rocks and stones, lending a desolate appearance to the area. The plain stretches
onwards in all directions, though to the north there are also some small shrubs
and stunted trees.
name: Sparse Plain

desc:
These fields are covered by weeds and grasses, as well as a few scattered bushes
and shrubs that add texture to the view. Further south, the land takes on a
healthier appearance, the grass growing greener and taller, the bushes wider and
thicker. A decaying building lies to the east, but there's no entrance on this
side.
name: Broken Fields

desc:
Life seems to flourish abundantly in this tranquil corner of the countryside,
where nature's symphony fills the air with its harmonious melodies. The verdant
landscape is adorned with a thick carpet of lush, green grass, a testament to
the vitality of the earth. Amongst this verdant expanse, an array of small
flowers blooms in a kaleidoscope of colours, adding splashes of vibrancy to the
scene.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
The plain stretches out into the distance, in all directions. The grass exhibits
varying shades of green; while lush in this vicinity, it appears drier and less
vibrant as one heads further south, transitioning to a yellow and somewhat
wilted appearance toward the east.
name: Green Plain

desc:
A decaying building lies to the north and west of this abandoned field. The roof
of the building, apparently planks of wood, does not look in great shape, the
wood rotting, and probably leaky when it rains. The field here is grass, fairly
healthy looking, in contrast with the grass to the east. Similar grassland lies
to the south.
name: Abandoned Field near a Building

desc:
The plain here is rather dry and dead, with a few sickly patches of grass the
only life to be seen. This land might once have flourished with life, but all
has become barren with the passing of the days since a significant river cut its
way down into a gulch, taking the water further and further away. The deep gulch
passes to the southwest of here. To the west, it is possible to climb down into
the chasm, but to the south it has become so deep that it would be necessary to
search out handholds with great care.
name: Dry Plain

desc:
The gulch cuts a deep, twisting pathway through the plain, where once a seasonal
stream meandered. The waterway has long ago dried up, to leave only some shrubs
and a deep chasm, with steep high walls of stones, many of them precariously
left behind during previous floods due to the bend here. Some of the shrubs have
taken root down here, while the roots of one of the others above hang down into
the gulch.
name: Shadowed Gulch

desc:
Cleaving a deep gouge through the wilderness, the gulch turns here from a
westerly to a southerly course. The stone walls are cold and smooth, rising
vertically for many feet. A few hardy shrubs reach upwards, striving for the
light of the sun.
name: Shadowed Gulch

desc:
Smooth stone walls trace the path of this deep gulch through the wilderness.
Rising tall, they seem to offer no hand or footholds, and like the ground
underfoot, they are made predominantly of cold, hard rock. High on the south
wall, a thin covering of earth can be seen, through which a few coarse roots
poke.
name: Deep Gulch

desc:
The gulch cleaves a mighty cleft, from east to west, through the land. The
smooth stone walls rise high above the bottom, with no visible handholds to make
an ascent easier. This place is deeply shaded, with nothing much growing in this
dried river bed, though a tree branch, bearing brown, dried-out leaves, dangles
down from above out of reach.
name: Deep Gulch

desc:
This circular chamber of dirt and rock has been hollowed out from the
surrounding earth. A latern hangs from a hook in the eastern wall, giving a
pale, dancing light. A thick door is framed in the northern wall of the chamber.
name: Hollowed Chamber

desc:
The excavated tunnels form a junction here, with passages leading out to the
west, east, and south. A large lantern hangs from the ceiling, almost like a
chandelier. Wooden supports keep the keep the ceiling from caving in, though by
the look of them that may not be for long.
name: Junction in the Caverns

desc:
A large passage has been cut through the earth and rock here. Rubble lines the
edges of the cavern, leftovers from the mining. A few wooden braces are all that
keep the walls from collapsing. A few torches sit in iron sconces, giving
flickering light to the bending cavern.
name: Excavated Passageway

desc:
A thick layer of packed mud coats the walls of this chamber. A path through the
mud, beaten down by the tread of countless boots, leads to the west and the
north. A few torches are tied to wooden posts and stuck into the floor, casting
warm glow over the wet earth.
name: Mud-walled Chamber

desc:
This cavern has been made into an office of sorts, complete with desk, cabinets
and a chair. Stacks of ledgers, maps, and other papers lie on crates and barrels
around the chamber. A couple lanterns are set in brass holders in the wall,
providing plenty of light by which to work.
name: Makeshift Office

desc:
Crates, jars, barrels, and sacks cover almost every inch of this cavern. A few
shelves have been set into the dirt walls, though they are covered in a thick
layer of grime. A small brass lantern hangs from the ceiling, provide a dim
light to illuminate the mess.
name: Cluttered Storage Area

desc:
The dry plain stretches out to the west and south as far as the eye can see,
with sparse vegetation and short, coarse grass underfoot. A steep-sided gulch
cuts through the terrain to the north, north-east and east. Scrubby vegetation
lines its banks, with one particularly tall shrub to the north.
name: Dry Plain near a Gulch

desc:
The plains are covered by weeds and tall clumps of unhealthy yellowing grasses.
The monotony of the scene is relieved by a few small bushes and a scattering of
small boulders. To the north, the land looks more fertile judging by the colour
of the grass.
name: Unhealthy Plain

desc:
The plain seems to stretch endlessly in all directions. The grass here appears
yellow and sickly, but further to the south the land seems even more
unwholesome. To the west, the plain looks greener. Here and there, an anthill is
visible amongst the grass.
name: Unhealthy Plain

desc:
Though this plain is dry, it is covered with grass and light vegetation. To the
east, a road cleaves the parched plain, which appears to be even more desolate
on the far side of the thoroughfare. The plain continues to the north, and also
the south and west where the dry grass looks yellow and sickly.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
Tall grass, rather dry looking, occupies this area and as far east and south as
the horizon, not terribly far away. Some small, hardy bushes cling to life. To
the north, abandoned fields are visible, while to the west this plain looks
unhealthily yellow.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
An arid plain stretches both north and south, sandwiched between a north-south
road to the west and a north-south gulch to the east. Seemingly, this was once
the course of a notable waterway, which has long ago dried up. Scattered hardy
shrubs can be seen at the lip of the gulch.
name: Arid Plain near a Road

desc:
The plain is rather dry and desolate. There is little vegetation, with the
exception of some short grass. Some small anthills further add to the arid
appearance. To the west, there is a road running north and south. To the north,
east and south, the dry plain continues.
name: Dry Plain
